prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response:
One song that never fails to move me is **"Hallelujah" by Leonard Cohen**.  

It works on me not just because of its hauntingly beautiful melody or Cohen’s weathered voice, but because of how it balances spiritual longing with raw human imperfection. The lyrics weave biblical imagery with intimate, vulnerable moments — blending the sacred and the profane — so that the "hallelujah" feels earned through both joy and sorrow, faith and doubt. When I listen, it doesn’t ask me to choose one emotion; instead, it holds the tension of brokenness and beauty in one breath. That bittersweet honesty makes it feel like a prayer for anyone who’s ever loved, lost, or simply tried to make sense of life’s contradictions.
